sections.html:
Regional Style · Netherlands

Dutch Design

Bold primary colors, sharp geometric forms, confident typography, and the playful-yet-structured spirit of a nation that gave the world De Stijl and Mondrian — Dutch Design translates centuries of graphic excellence into digital form. The bright blues and oranges, razor-sharp edges, and generous negative space create interfaces that feel simultaneously experimental and meticulously organized.

This bold geometric style influences design studios, cultural institutions, architecture firms, and creative agencies worldwide. You'll see its DNA in museum websites, design festival programs, Dutch government portals, and avant-garde editorial layouts — anywhere that values the intersection of functional clarity and creative boldness that defines the Netherlands' design heritage.
